rupture.definition: A break or crack in a relationship caused by something hurtful.
rupture.example: Every family experiences rupture, but repair is what matters.

repair.definition: Fixing something that was broken or damaged.
repair.example: Saying sorry is one way to repair a relationship.

harshly.example: I wish I never spoke harshly to my child.

unjustified.definition: Not having a good reason; unfair.
unjustified.example: My parents never admitted to being unjustified.

apologized.definition: Said sorry for doing something wrong.
apologized.example: My parents never apologized for treating me unfairly.

unfairly.definition: In a way that is not right or equal.
unfairly.example: She was treated unfairly but no one said sorry.

mistaken.example: Even when proved to be mistaken, they never apologized.

conscious.definition: Done on purpose; aware of what you are doing.
conscious.example: She made a conscious decision not to repeat her parents' mistakes.

intentions.definition: Plans or wishes about what you want to do.
intentions.example: Despite her good intentions, she sometimes made mistakes.

regretted.definition: Felt sorry about something you did.
regretted.example: She behaved in a way she regretted.

realized.example: When she realized her mistake, she apologized.

confessed.example: They confessed to their daughter when they slipped up.

slipped.definition: Made a small mistake without meaning to.

experiment.definition: Trying something new to see what happens.
experiment.example: It was an experiment in creating a new emotional family pattern.

link.example: She was creating a new link in the emotional family chain.

grumpy.definition: In a bad mood; easily annoyed.
grumpy.example: Sorry I was grumpy in the car, Mum.

reflecting.example: She was reflecting on her own behavior.

perceived.definition: Seen or understood in a certain way.
perceived.example: She tried to repair a perceived rupture.

thrilled.example: I was thrilled when she said sorry on her own.

sensitive.definition: Aware of and caring about other people's feelings.
sensitive.example: Being sensitive to feelings helps relationships.

stand-offs.definition: Situations where two sides refuse to give in.
stand-offs.example: Repair is always better than stand-offs.

battlegrounds.definition: Places or situations full of fighting and conflict.

tantrum.definition: A sudden outburst of anger, especially by a child.
tantrum.example: She learned to talk about her feelings instead of having a tantrum.

annoying.definition: Making you feel slightly angry or bothered.
annoying.example: Yes, it is really annoying, isn't it?

acting out.definition: Expressing feelings through bad behavior instead of words.
acting out.example: Instead of acting out on her anger, she put it into words.
